_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot — TX state batch)
_meta.note: TX SD-3 Nichols, SD-5 Schwertner, HD-4 Bell, HD-5 Hefner (state tier); plus roster-currency fix for SD-4 (was VACANT after Creighton's 2025-10-02 resignation, now Brett Ligon R, sworn 2026-05-19). Evidence from TX Legislature roll calls, Texas Alliance for Life / Texas Right to Life / Texas Values Action scorecards, The Texan & KXAN voucher vote rosters, NRA/TSRA ratings, dated quotes. HIGH-VALUE CATCH: Nichols is a documented GOP outlier — lone Senate R against school choice (SB2) in BOTH 2023 & 2025, and TX Right to Life SUSPENDED his endorsement over his rape-exception pledge; scored down accordingly. Cells with only party-inference or no cited record left null.

profile.background: TX Senate SD-3 (East Texas) since 2007; former Jacksonville mayor and Texas Transportation Commissioner. Most senior Senate Republican. Announced 2025-06-24 he will NOT seek re-election; serves through Jan 2027.

robert-nichols@TX.notes_set: TX State Senator, SD-3 (R), since 2007 — RETIRING (announced 2025-06-24, not seeking re-election; serves through Jan 2027; open-seat GOP primary 2026). [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] A genuine GOP outlier (YCT lowest-scoring Senate R; chronic F on the fiscal index). Pro-life on the 2021 bans (Yea SB8 Heartbeat + HB1280 trigger; Texas Alliance for Life 100%) BUT publicly pledged a rape exception, prompting Texas Right to Life to SUSPEND its endorsement, and voted 'Present' on the 2025 chemical-abortion-pill ban — so he is NOT an abortion abolitionist (sanctity q1 FALSE). MARQUEE DEVIATION: the ONLY Senate Republican to vote AGAINST school choice/ESAs (SB2) in BOTH 2023 and 2025, calling vouchers 'a new form of segregation' (family q0 FALSE). Conservative elsewhere: backed the 2025 'What is a Woman' act + drag restriction (SB18), Ten Commandments/prayer-in-schools (SB10/SB11), SB1 election integrity, SB4 (2023) border enforcement, and HB1927 constitutional carry. Dimensions without an individually-cited vote left null.

0.note: Voted Yea on SB8 (2021 Heartbeat Act, 19-12) and HB1280 (trigger ban); Texas Alliance for Life 100% on the 2025 (89th) scorecard.

1.note: Publicly pledged to support adding a rape exception to the abortion ban; Texas Right to Life SUSPENDED its endorsement — not an abortion abolitionist.

2.note: Supported HB229 ('What is a Woman Act', biological-sex definition) per Texas Values Action 2025.

4.note: Supported SB18 (2025) restricting drag/'Drag Time Story Hour' performances before minors.

0.note: The ONLY Senate Republican to vote AGAINST school choice/ESAs (SB2) in both 2023 and 2025; called vouchers 'a new form of segregation.'

3.note: Supported SB10 (Ten Commandments in schools) and SB11 (prayer in schools), 2025.
Voted for SB1 (2021 Election Integrity Act: banned drop boxes, 24-hr/drive-thru voting, ballot-harvesting penalties); campaign touts it.

1.note: In the GOP majority passing SB4 (2023) state illegal-entry/arrest-and-removal law (Senate 17-11, party-line).

0.note: Publicly backed HB1927 (2021 permitless/constitutional carry): 'the people of SD-3 overwhelmingly support this'; bill passed Senate 18-13.

profile.background: TX Senate SD-5 (Georgetown/Bryan) since 2013; orthopedic surgeon; chairs Senate Business & Commerce. Won the 2026 GOP primary; faces Democrat Paul Thomasson in November 2026.

charles-schwertner@TX.notes_set: TX State Senator, SD-5 (R), since 2013 — sitting, won the 2026 GOP primary, general candidate Nov 2026. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Reliable conservative with no cross-party deviation found: co-sponsored SB8 (2021 Heartbeat) with Texas Alliance for Life 100%; voted Yea on SB14 (2023 ban on minor gender-transition, 19-12 party-line); voted Yes on the 2023 (SB1) and 2025 (SB2) school-choice/ESA bills; AUTHORED HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry) and SB21 (2025 Texas Strategic Bitcoin Reserve); voted for SB1 (2021 election integrity) and SB4 (2023 border enforcement). Dimensions without an individually-cited vote (Christian liberty, public justice, federal-overreach) left null.

0.note: Co-sponsored SB8 (2021 Heartbeat Act); Texas Alliance for Life 100% on the 2025 (89th) scorecard.

2.note: Voted Yea on SB14 (2023) banning gender-transition procedures for minors (Senate 19-12 party-line).

0.note: Voted Yes on SB1 (2023 special session) and SB2 (2025) ESA/school-choice bills.
Authored SB21 (2025) creating the Texas Strategic Bitcoin Reserve — supports decentralized crypto as a state asset.

3.note: Voted Yes on SB1 (2021 Election Integrity Act): banned drop boxes, 24-hr/drive-thru voting, harvesting penalties.

1.note: Voted Yes on SB4 (2023) making illegal entry a state crime and authorizing arrest/removal (Senate party-line).

0.note: AUTHORED HB1927 (2021), Texas permitless/constitutional carry — his signature bill; NRA/TSRA A+.

profile.religion: Baptist — First Baptist Church of Forney
profile.background: TX House HD-4 (Kaufman/Henderson) since 2019; electrical contractor and rancher; former Forney ISD school-board president; chairs House General Investigating (89th). Won the 2026 GOP primary; faces Democrat Mark Moseley in November 2026.

keith-bell@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-4 (R), since 2019 — sitting, won the 2026 GOP primary, general candidate Nov 2026. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Strong on life/family/guns/elections/border by ratings + recorded votes: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025), Texas Values Action 93% 'Faith & Family Champion'; voted Yea SB12 (2023 drag-restriction, co-sponsor); co-author of SB1 (2021 election integrity) and voted Yes on SB1070 (2023 ERIC withdrawal); voted Yes HB9 (2021 border-security appropriations) and HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry, NRA Life member). NUANCE: voted to STRIP the ESA/voucher provision in 2023 (one of ~21 House R defections) but voted FOR the ESA bill SB2 in 2025 — scored on his current (2025) pro-school-choice vote with the 2023 flip documented. Fiscal grade is only a C- (TFR 2023). Dimensions without an individually-cited vote left null.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025); self-states he voted for SB8 (Heartbeat) and HB1280 (trigger ban).

4.note: Voted Yea (and co-sponsored) SB12 (2023) restricting drag/sexually-oriented performances before minors; Texas Values Action 93% 'Faith & Family Champion.'

0.note: Voted Yea on final passage of SB2 (2025 ESA/school-choice). NOTE: had voted to strip ESAs in 2023, then supported the 2025 bill.

3.note: Co-author of SB1 (2021 omnibus election-integrity law): mail-ballot ID, banned drive-thru/24-hr voting, harvesting penalties.

4.note: Voted Yes on SB1070 (2023) withdrawing Texas from the ERIC multistate voter-data compact — state sovereignty over election data.

0.note: Voted Yes on HB9 (2021) ~$1.8B border-security appropriations (barrier/Operation Lone Star buildup).
Voted Yes on HB1927 (2021 permitless/constitutional carry); NRA Life member.

profile.background: TX House HD-5 (Upshur/Wood, East Texas) since 2017; businessman from Mount Pleasant, father of seven; chairs House Homeland Security, Public Safety & Veterans' Affairs (89th). Won the 2026 GOP primary.

cole-hefner@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-5 (R), since 2017 — sitting, won the 2026 GOP primary (Abbott-endorsed). [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Strong conservative: Texas Right to Life 100% + Texas Alliance for Life 100% (89th/2025); voted FOR SB2 (2025 ESA/school-choice) and publicly defended it; publicly backed SB1 (2021 election integrity, 'easy to vote and hard to cheat'); authored HB17/SB17 (2025) banning hostile-foreign (China/Russia/Iran/NK) land & farmland ownership and chaired the SB17 conference; co-author of HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry) and NRA 'Defender of Freedom' awardee. NOTE: only a C-/F fiscal grade in 2023 (Texas Scorecard) — a fiscal knock from the right, not a left deviation. Marriage/Christian-liberty session votes (SB14, SB10/SB11) were not individually roll-call-confirmed and are left null rather than inferred.

0.note: Texas Right to Life 100% and Texas Alliance for Life 100% on the 2025 (89th) scorecards.
Voted FOR SB2 (2025 ESA/school-choice) and publicly defended it ('what works for one child may not work for a sibling').

3.note: Publicly backed SB1 (2021 election-integrity package): 'easy to vote and hard to cheat... a uniform statewide framework' (dated quote).

4.note: Authored HB17/SB17 (2025) prohibiting land & farmland purchases by entities tied to China, Russia, Iran, North Korea; chaired the SB17 conference committee.

0.note: Co-author of HB1927 (2021 permitless/constitutional carry); received NRA's 'Defender of Freedom' award (2021).

profile.background: TX Senate SD-4 (Houston/Montgomery County area); former Montgomery County District Attorney. Won the 2026-05-02 special election (~75%) to fill the seat left by Brandon Creighton's 2025-10-02 resignation; sworn in 2026-05-19. Faces a November 2026 rematch with Ron Angeletti (D) for the full term.

vacant@TX.notes_set: ROSTER-CURRENCY FIX [2026-06 national refinement]: SD-4 was labeled VACANT — it was vacant only between Brandon Creighton's resignation (2025-10-02, to become Texas Tech University System chancellor) and the special election. Brett Ligon (R), former Montgomery County DA, won the 2026-05-02 special election (~75% vs. Ron Angeletti D) and was sworn in 2026-05-19. He faces a Nov-2026 rematch with Angeletti for the full four-year term. Seated only weeks ago, so he has essentially no SD-4 roll-call record yet — scored nothing (confidence roster_verified pending a deep refinement of his DA-era and early-Senate record). FOLLOW-UP: the database slug is still 'vacant' and should be renamed to 'brett-ligon'.
